title: SAP and Google Cloud deploy agentic commerce architecture

raw_body:
SAP and Google Cloud are deploying agentic commerce architecture to automate multi-agent marketing and retail operations at enterprise scale. SAP research indicates 78 percent of businesses consider AI essential for retaining customers in 2026. However, the same data reveals fewer than two in five companies share customer data across customer experience (37%) or CRM (39%) […]
The post SAP and Google Cloud deploy agentic commerce architecture appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: SAP and Google Cloud are introducing agentic commerce architecture to enhance marketing and retail automation on a large scale. According to SAP, 78% of businesses view AI as crucial for customer retention by 2026. Yet, less than 40% of companies effectively share customer data across their operations. This highlights a significant gap in leveraging AI for customer engagement, making the deployment of this architecture timely and essential.
why_it_matters: Businesses aiming to retain customers will benefit from improved automation and data sharing mechanisms, potentially increasing their competitiveness.
This development signals a broader shift towards integrating AI in retail and marketing, showcasing the industry's need for better data utilization.

lenses.eli12: SAP and Google Cloud are working together to create a system that helps businesses automate their marketing and sales processes. Imagine a smart assistant that can handle multiple tasks at once, making things easier for companies. This matters because it could help businesses keep their customers happy and engaged more effectively.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this means a push towards integrating AI into their operations to meet customer needs. The focus on automation could lead to cost savings and improved efficiency in marketing efforts. Companies not leveraging this technology might fall behind in customer retention.
lenses.engineer: The agentic commerce architecture aims to automate multi-agent systems for marketing and retail, enhancing operational efficiency. With 78% of businesses identifying AI as essential for customer retention, the architecture could optimize data sharing processes, which currently sees only 37% of companies sharing customer experience data. This presents an opportunity for improved analytics and decision-making.
